NAME: Site Name
DESCRIPTION:
A unique name for a site that corresponds to a georeferenced point.
Examples include: “101”,“DFTC”, “Site 1”, “99AK180003”

NAME: Profile/Plot Name
DESCRIPTION:
A unique name for a single profile. This can be the same as the site name if there is only one profile at the site. For NRCS data it is the same as “Pedon ID”.
Examples include: “BZBS4”, “99P0544”, “Profile 1”

NAME: Layer Name
DESCRIPTION:
A unique name for a single sampled layer. This can be a name that denotes depth, sequence, etc.
Examples include: “0-10”, “10-20”, “40A04456”, “BZBS4.1”

NAME: Laboratory Layer Number
DESCRIPTION: Laboratory layer sample identifier if different than the Layer Name (data contributor’s own identifier).

NAME: Labeled Addition
DESCRIPTION:
Was this layer/gas/fraction/sample isotopically labeled? Answer yes or leave blank.
Controlled vocabulary: yes_blank

NAME: Layer Top
DESCRIPTION: The top (upper) depth of the layer. The surface of the non-green (i.e. non-living) surface layer is “0”. The top of the O-horizon should be 0.

NAME: Layer Bottom
DESCRIPTION: The bottom (lower) depth of the layer. If it is uncertain that the bottom of the designated horizon was reached, enter the depth to the bottom of the sampled layer. Note that this is the same as "hzn_bot" in the NRCS database.

NAME: Horizon Designation
DESCRIPTION:
Follow conventions of the USDA-NRCS-NSSC Field Book for Describing and Sampling Soils (Staff 2002; pp. 2-2 through 2-4). Note that datasets originally using another convention will be modified for this column. If a different convention was used it can be entered in Horizon Designation Other.
Examples include: “Oi”, “A”, “AE”, “A/E”, “Bwf”, “Bhs”

NAME: Horizon Designation Other
DESCRIPTION:
Horizon designation following a convention other than NRCS. Please document the convention in the associated Horizon Designation Other Note.
Examples include: “L”, “F”, “M”, “H”, “OF”, “OM”, “OH”

NAME: Horizon Designation Other Note
DESCRIPTION: Provide a reference for the horizon designations employed if they differ from NRCS standards in the USDA-NRCS-NSSC Field Book for Describing and Sampling Soils (Staff 2002).

NAME: Soil Layer Notes
DESCRIPTION:
A description of the sampled layer. For example, a full NRCS horizon description.
Examples include: “2-5 cm; brown (10YR 4/3) silt loam; moderate very fine platy structure; very friable; many roots; many fine vesicular pores; brown (10YR 5/3) on plate faces; many very thin bands of finer material similar to B't (below) throughout the horizon; abrupt wavy boundary”, “Denser, darker fabrics with white fungus and larger roots”

NAME: Bulk Layer Carbon Analysis Method
DESCRIPTION:
Provide reference or describe the sample preparation and analysis methods used to determine carbon concentrations, whether Total Carbon, organic C, or Loss on Ignition.
Examples include: “Determined total %C from CHN analysis of air dried <2mm fraction”

NAME: Moist Munsell Color
DESCRIPTION:
Color of moist soil based on the Munsell soil color chart.
Examples include: “10YR 3/3”

NAME: Evidence of Burning
DESCRIPTION:
Descriptive information indicating evidence of burning within the layer.
Examples include: “charcoal”, “burned organic matter”, “ash”

NAME: Bulk Density Method
DESCRIPTION:
Please reference or describe the methods used to determine bulk density.
Examples include: “Determined BD_sample from core volume and dry weight of <2mm fraction”, “Strahm et al. 2005 Soil Solution…”

NAME: Bulk Density, Coarse Fragments Removed
DESCRIPTION: Grams of oven-dried soil per cubic centimeter, with soil particles greater than 2 mm and roots greater that 1 cm diameter removed.

NAME: Bulk Density With Coarse Fragments
DESCRIPTION: Grams of oven-dried soil per cubic centimeter, with soil particles greater than 2 mm and roots greater that 1 cm diameter included.

NAME: Bulk Density Minus Estimated Coarse Fragments
DESCRIPTION: Grams of oven-dried soil per cubic centimeter, with the content of soil particles >2mm and roots >1cm diameter estimated and subtracted.

NAME: Bulk Density Other
DESCRIPTION: Grams of oven-dried soil per cubic centimeter. Please document the method used in the associated Bulk Density Method including the soil particle fraction used.

NAME: pH Method
DESCRIPTION: Provide reference or describe the sample preparation and analysis methods used for determinations of pH if not 1:1 soil and distilled water paste or in CaCl2.

NAME: Soil pH CaCl2
DESCRIPTION: 1:2 soil-CaCl2 is the pH of a sample measured in 0.01M CaCl2 at a 1:2 soil:solution ratio.

NAME: Soil pH 1:1
DESCRIPTION: 1:1 distilled water and soil paste. If pH was done by a different method, then enter it into one of the other soil pH fields.

NAME: Soil pH Other
DESCRIPTION: pH measurements other than 1:1 soil and distilled water paste or in CaCl2. Please document the method in the associated pH Method.

NAME: CaCO3
DESCRIPTION: Inorganic carbon concentration as measured or estimated by the contributor. Please document the method in the associated Processed Site Organic Carbon Content Method.

NAME: Percent Sand
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of soil particles greater than 0.05 mm in the sample remaining after removal of particles greater than 2 mm and roots greater than 1 cm diameter. See Gee, G.W. & Bauder, J.W. 1986.

NAME: Percent Silt
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of soil particles in the size range from 0.002 to 0.050 mm in the sample remaining after removal of particles greater than 2 mm and roots greater than 1 cm diameter. See Gee, G.W. & Bauder, J.W. 1986.

NAME: Percent Clay
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of soil particles less than 0.002 mm in the sample remaining after removal of particles greater than 2 mm and roots greater than 1 cm diameter. See Gee, G.W. & Bauder, J.W. 1986.

NAME: Coarse Fragments Method
DESCRIPTION: Provide a reference for or describe the methods used to determine coarse fragment content.

NAME: Coarse Fragments
DESCRIPTION: The weight fraction of particles with >2 mm diameter is reported as a gravimetric percent on a whole soil base. Please include metadata in the Coarse Fragments Method column (e.g. estimate or quantitative).

NAME: Cation Exchange Capacity
DESCRIPTION: Cation Exchange Capacity. Document the extractant solution in the metadata worksheet, Lab Analysis Method.

NAME: Gravimentric % Moisture
DESCRIPTION: Gravimetric water content of the sampled layer.

NAME: Volumetric % Moisture
DESCRIPTION: Water content of the soil sample expressed as a percent of total sample volume.

NAME: Extractable Al, Fe, Mn units
DESCRIPTION: Controlled vocabulary: extract_units

NAME: Base cation extraction units
DESCRIPTION: Controlled vocabulary: extract_units

NAME: CEC and acidity units
DESCRIPTION: Controlled vocabulary: extract_units

NAME: Extractable metals units
DESCRIPTION: Controlled vocabulary: extract_units

NAME: Extractable P units
DESCRIPTION: Controlled vocabulary: extract_units

NAME: Bulk Layer Calculated Soil Organic Carbon Stock
DESCRIPTION: The data contributor's calculated soil organic carbon stock value for the site, cluster, profile or layer.

NAME: Bulk Layer Calculated Soil Organic Carbon Stock Type
DESCRIPTION:
An indication of which portion of the profile (organic, mineral, organic + mineral) is described by the contributor's calculated soil organic carbon stock at the site, cluster, profile or layer level.
Controlled vocabulary: ccon_type

NAME: Bulk Layer Calculated Soil Organic Carbon Stock Standard Deviation
DESCRIPTION: The standard deviation of the site, cluster, profile, or layer-level calculated soil organic carbon stock. (For layer soil organic carbon stocks, this may be based on analytical or sampling replicates of %C or bulk density measurements).

NAME: Bulk Layer Calculated Soil Organic Carbon Stock Method
DESCRIPTION: A description of the methods used to calculate (including any gap-filling) site, cluster, profile or layer soil organic carbon stocks.

NAME: Bulk Layer Total Carbon
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of carbon in the dried, milled soil sample. This measurement will typically correspond to analytical results from an elemental analyzer.

NAME: Bulk Layer Organic Carbon
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of carbon in a dried soil sample after acidification with HCl, OR organic carbon as estimated by Walkley-Black Modified Acid-Dichromate (e.g. ‘Organic Carbon’ in the NRCS database). Please document the method in the associated Carbon Analysis Methods.

NAME: Bulk Layer Total Nitrogen
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of nitrogen (organic and inorganic) in an oven-dried sample (the laboratory analytical concentration).

NAME: Bulk Layer C:N
DESCRIPTION: Mass ratio of total carbon to total nitrogen, as calculated from the total carbon and total nitrogen concentrations of the bulk layer, fraction, or other sample type.

NAME: Bulk Layer Loss on Ignition
DESCRIPTION: Percent by weight of the organic content of the <2mm fraction is the organic material lost after ignition. It is reported on a <2 mm base. Please document the method in the associated Carbon Analysis Method.

NAME: Root Quantity and Size
DESCRIPTION:
Use USDA NRCS conventions from the USDA-NRCS-NSSC Field Book for Describing and Sampling Soils (Staff 2002; p.2-56): Record the average quantity from 3 to 5 representative unit areas. Size classes of roots being considered: very fine, < 1mm; fine, 1 – 2mm diameter.
Examples include: "few very fine", "Common very fine”, "fine"

NAME: <2mm Root Mass
DESCRIPTION: Dry weight of roots ≤2 mm in diameter in the sample.

NAME: Bulk Layer δ15N
DESCRIPTION: Per mille signature of δ15N relative to air (international standard).

NAME: Bulk Layer δ13C
DESCRIPTION: Per mille signature of δ13C relative to Pee Dee Belemnite.

NAME: Radiocarbon Laboratory ID
DESCRIPTION: Laboratory code for radiocarbon laboratory. Complete list of past and present laboratory codes can found published in Radiocarbon in November 2011 (http://www.radiocarbon.org/Info/labcodes.html).

NAME: Radiocarbon Laboratory Sample Number
DESCRIPTION: Layer sample identifier as designated by the radiocarbon laboratory.

NAME: Radiocarbon Analysis Year
DESCRIPTION: Year in which radiocarbon analysis was performed on the sample.

NAME: Bulk Layer Δ14C
DESCRIPTION: Per mille signature of Δ14C relative to NBS Oxalic Acid standard.

NAME: Bulk Layer Δ14C Standard Deviation
DESCRIPTION: Error estimate for the fraction (eg. standard deviation of analytical reps).

NAME: Bulk Layer Uncalibrated Radiocarbon Age
DESCRIPTION: Uncalibrated radiocarbon age of bulk soil in layer, as calculated from corrected fraction modern, using the Libby half-life value of 5568 years.

NAME: Bulk Layer Uncalibrated Radiocarbon Age Standard Deviation
DESCRIPTION: Error estimate for the fraction (eg. standard deviation of analytical reps).

NAME: Bulk Layer Fraction Modern
DESCRIPTION: Deviation of the bulk sample or fraction from modern. Modern is defined as 95% of the radiocarbon concentration (in AD 1950) of NBS Oxalic Acid standard, 13C-corrected.

NAME: Bulk Layer Fraction Modern Standard Deviation
DESCRIPTION: Error estimate for an individual layer (eg. standard deviation of analytical reps).

NAME: Texture Class
DESCRIPTION: Soil texture classification. If no information is provided, this will be automatically generated from %sand, %silt, %clay data (coarse = ≥50% sand; fine = <50% sand).
